widgets_values:
ATLAS MASTER DMP — SUBGRAPH EDITION

The entire layer rig (solve + shared depth + SAM3 sky + sky dome + bg band mask + bg/fg clean plates,
with the calibrated 384/1.5 band settings, fill_occluded, edge mattes, sky edge-extend + frame outpaint)
lives inside the single 'Atlas DMP Rig' subgraph node. Its widget panel is the PRESET SURFACE — the only
choices that vary per shot: SAM prompt, depth models (set BOTH to indoor for interiors), the band split
(bg near_pct and fg far_pct must match), and the sky card tuning. Double-click the node to open the rig
and reach everything else.

Kept in the main graph, per design: 🧭 Assess Image (VLM pre-flight gate — pauses everything until
▶ Continue, per-image approvals), both Atlas Viewports, the Qwen novel-view subgraph, AddPatchView,
the occlusion-mask QA corner (its angle now auto-follows 📐 Extract Angle via patch_view_override),
and the Nuke + Maya all-in-one layer exports.

FLOW: Queue → read assessment → set rig presets → ▶ Continue → layers build + viewport 1 renders →
orbit to your move's end frame → 📐 Extract Angle → the Qwen patch generates and viewport 2 +
both DCC exports run automatically. The patch branch stays paused until an extraction exists for THIS
image (fingerprinted), and the assess gate re-arms on image swap.

nodes.title: 🧭 Assess Image — VLM gate (pauses until ▶ Continue)
Assessment — apply to the rig presets, then ▶ Continue
Occlusion QA — angle auto-follows 📐 Extract Angle
Add Patch View — angle from 📐 Extract Angle
SAM3 on the PATCH image — sky out of the patch mesh

groups.title: ① Atlas DMP Rig — ALL layer plumbing in one node (double-click to open)

nodes.title: TextEncodeQwenImageEditPlus (Positive)
widgets_values: Change the furniture leather difference in image 1 to the fur material in image 2.

nodes.title: KSampler settings
widgets_values:
You can test and find the best setting by yourself. The following table is for reference.
|   | Qwen | Comfy | lightning LoRA |
|--------|---------|------------|---------------------------|
| Steps  | 40      | 20         | 4                         |
| CFG    | 4.0     | 4.0       | 1.0                       |

By default, we use 20 steps as we just don't want it to take too long. Try 40 if you want a better result, but it will take longer.
